explanation: This command simulates a suspicious mshta.exe execution from a known suspicious folder (C:\Users\Public\), which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It is a direct test case for the rule.

This command simulates a suspicious mshta execution from a known suspicious folder (C:\Windows\Temp\), matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria. The omission of the .exe extension is allowed since the system may still resolve it correctly.
This command uses quotes to handle spaces in the path and executes mshta.exe from a suspicious folder (C:\ProgramData\), which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command uses a nested path within a suspicious folder (C:\Users\Public\) and executes mshta.exe, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command executes mshta.exe from a suspicious folder (C:\Windows\Temp\), which is flagged by the Sigma rule. The path is nested to simulate realistic obfuscation techniques.
